skill.name: architecture
skill.description: Architectural decision-making framework. Requirements analysis, trade-off evaluation, ADR documentation. Use when making architecture decisions or analyzing system design.
skill.summary: Framework for making architectural decisions with trade-off analysis and ADR documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation guide for architectural decision-making. Detected patterns are markdown formatting (backticks), authentication standards (SAML, JWT), and normal decision-making terms (validate). No actual security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Block Delimiters
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected backtick characters in markdown files. These are code block delimiters in documentation, not shell execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backticks are markdown code fences (```yaml, ```) in documentation files - legitimate formatting, not code execution

low_findings.title: SAML Authentication Reference
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'SAML' as related to Windows SAM database. SAML is Security Assertion Markup Language, a standard authentication protocol.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) is a standard enterprise SSO protocol, not Windows Security Account Manager

low_findings.title: JWT Authentication Reference
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'JWT' as weak cryptographic algorithm. JWT is JSON Web Token, a standard authentication mechanism.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: JWT (JSON Web Token) is an industry-standard authentication mechanism, not a weak cryptographic algorithm

low_findings.title: Decision Validation Terms
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'validate' as system reconnaissance. These are normal architectural decision validation steps.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 'Validate' refers to validating architectural decisions in decision trees, not system reconnaissance

content.value_statement: This skill helps teams make better architectural decisions through structured trade-off analysis and ADR documentation. It provides decision trees, pattern guidance, and templates to document architecture choices with their rationale.
actual_capabilities: Guide requirements discovery with structured questions
Provide decision trees for architectural pattern selection
Generate ADR templates with trade-off documentation
Compare architectural patterns with usage guidance
Classify projects (MVP, SaaS, Enterprise) and recommend approaches
Evaluate architecture decisions against team constraints
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files
Does not analyze existing codebase structure

use_cases.title: New Project Architecture Planning
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, use the skill to determine appropriate architecture based on team size, scale requirements, and timeline constraints.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and architects planning new applications

use_cases.title: Documenting Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: When making significant architectural choices, use the ADR templates to record context, options considered, decision rationale, and trade-offs accepted.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams needing to document design decisions

use_cases.title: Pattern Selection Guidance
use_cases.description: When uncertain about which architectural pattern to use, consult the decision trees to evaluate trade-offs between options like monolith vs microservices, REST vs GraphQL.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Requirements for New Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define the architecture for a new [project type] with [team size] developers, targeting [user scale] users, with a timeline of [timeline]. Budget is [budget constraint]. Use the architecture skill to guide this decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project and need architecture guidance

prompt_templates.title: Create Architecture Decision Record
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an ADR for choosing [technology/pattern] over alternatives. The context involves [problem description]. Consider these constraints: [list constraints]. Use the trade-off analysis framework from the architecture skill.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Microservices vs Monolith
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide between microservices and monolith architecture for a [project description] with [team size] developers. What are the trade-offs? When would each approach be justified?

prompt_templates.title: Select Data Access Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: What data access pattern would you recommend for a [project type] with [complexity level] data access needs? Consider: team size is [size], testing requirements are [level], data sources include [sources].

output_examples.input: Help me plan architecture for an MVP e-commerce app with 1 developer
output_examples.output: For a solo developer MVP, I recommend: Monolith structure with Next.js, Prisma for data access, JWT authentication, PostgreSQL database, Stripe for payments. Trade-offs: limited independent scaling, can extract services later when justified.

output_examples.input: Create ADR for choosing PostgreSQL over MongoDB
output_examples.output: ADR Template: Context - need reliable transactional data. Options considered: PostgreSQL (ACID, complex queries) vs MongoDB (flexible, horizontal scale). Decision: PostgreSQL. Rationale: E-commerce requires transactional integrity. Trade-offs accepted: Less flexible schema initially.

best_practices: Start with the simplest architecture that meets current requirements and add complexity only when proven necessary
Always document trade-offs - every architecture choice has costs and benefits that should be explicit
Use ADRs to capture not just what was decided but why, including constraints that influenced the choice
Consider team expertise when choosing patterns - the best pattern is useless if the team cannot maintain it
anti_patterns: Premature microservices - splitting services before team size or scale justifies the complexity
Over-abstraction with Clean/Hexagonal architecture when simple CRUD would suffice
Choosing CQRS or Event Sourcing without read/write performance evidence showing benefit
Ignoring trade-offs - every architectural choice has costs that must be acknowledged

faq.question: When should I use microservices instead of monolith?
faq.answer: Microservices are justified when: team exceeds 10 developers, different components need different scaling, clear domain boundaries exist. Start with modular monolith and extract services when proven necessary.

faq.question: What is an ADR and why do I need one?
faq.answer: ADR (Architecture Decision Record) documents significant decisions with context, options considered, the choice made, rationale, and trade-offs. It helps future developers understand why the system was designed a certain way.

faq.question: How do I determine the right architecture for my project?
faq.answer: Start with the project classification matrix: MVP (<1K users, solo dev), SaaS (1K-100K, 2-10 devs), Enterprise (100K+, 10+ devs). Match architecture complexity to actual constraints, not hypothetical future needs.

faq.question: When should I use the Repository pattern?
faq.answer: Use Repository pattern when: testing requires data source abstraction, multiple data sources exist, or data access logic is complex. For simple CRUD with single database, ORM direct access is simpler and sufficient.

faq.question: How do I evaluate trade-offs in architecture decisions?
faq.answer: For each option, document: specific pros, specific cons, complexity level, and conditions when that option is valid. Tie decisions to concrete constraints (team size, scale, timeline, budget) rather than abstract best practices.

faq.question: Can this skill write code for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides architectural guidance, decision frameworks, and documentation templates. It does not generate implementation code or modify files. Use it to plan and document before implementation.
